PREDICTION_UNIT: One row represents one student health-behavior profile. Predict whether the student's health condition is at-risk, unhealthy, or fit.
DOMAIN_CONTEXT: Student health-risk classification from synthetic tabular health and lifestyle data inspired by a college student health behavior dataset. Useful signal is likely in sleep duration and quality, heart rate, BMI, calorie expenditure, step count, exercise duration, water intake, diet type, stress level, physical activity level, smoking/alcohol behavior, gender, and missingness patterns. The training target is highly imbalanced toward at-risk, so balanced-class behavior and minority-class separability matter. A local source-style auxiliary dataset is available as student_health_dataset_50k.csv.gz with matching health/lifestyle feature names, external-only student_id and timestamp columns, and the same health_condition classes.
AVAILABLE_COLUMNS: Train columns: id, health_condition, sleep_duration, heart_rate, bmi, calorie_expenditure, step_count, exercise_duration, water_intake, diet_type, stress_level, sleep_quality, physical_activity_level, smoking_alcohol, gender. Test columns: id, sleep_duration, heart_rate, bmi, calorie_expenditure, step_count, exercise_duration, water_intake, diet_type, stress_level, sleep_quality, physical_activity_level, smoking_alcohol, gender. Submission columns: id, health_condition. Auxiliary student_health_dataset_50k.csv.gz columns: student_id, timestamp, sleep_duration, heart_rate, bmi, calorie_expenditure, step_count, exercise_duration, water_intake, diet_type, stress_level, sleep_quality, physical_activity_level, smoking_alcohol, gender, health_condition. Target classes are at-risk, unhealthy, and fit. Numeric columns include id, sleep_duration, heart_rate, bmi, calorie_expenditure, step_count, exercise_duration, and water_intake; the auxiliary dataset also has student_id. Categorical columns include diet_type, stress_level, sleep_quality, physical_activity_level, smoking_alcohol, and gender. Many competition feature columns contain missing values; the auxiliary source-style dataset is complete for its configured columns.
PREVIOUS_SOLUTION_MODES: No prior AIDE solution artifacts are assumed for this newly initialized project. Infer useful baseline and feature-search directions from the attached data schema, task description, and any exported run artifacts provided later.
VALIDATION_CONTEXT: Submissions are evaluated by balanced accuracy, higher is better. The target distribution is imbalanced, with at-risk as the majority class and fit/unhealthy as minority classes. Local validation and public leaderboard may diverge, so isolate one feature family at a time and watch minority-class recall rather than optimizing only majority-class accuracy.
LEAKAGE_CONSTRAINTS: Do not use health_condition except as the training label. Do not derive features from test labels or from submission labels. Train/test unsupervised feature engineering is allowed only when it does not use target labels. The auxiliary student_health_dataset_50k.csv.gz health_condition labels are external labels; use them only when the experiment explicitly assumes labeled public external data is allowed and the implementation avoids validation/test leakage. Do not assume auxiliary student_id matches competition id. Avoid target encoding or empirical target priors unless they are strictly fold-safe inside the generated solution.
EXPERIMENT_BUDGET: Each hypothesis must be verifiable as a single first experiment in under 1 hour on a server-class machine with about 200 GB RAM, 16 CPU cores, and one NVIDIA RTX 4090 GPU. Prefer compact feature blocks and bounded AutoGluon changes. Do not propose exhaustive searches, very large ensembles, or multi-hour training.
AUTOGLUON_PREPROCESS_EXAMPLE:
```python
def preprocess(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    out = df.copy(deep=True)
    numeric_cols = [
        "sleep_duration",
        "heart_rate",
        "bmi",
        "calorie_expenditure",
        "step_count",
        "exercise_duration",
        "water_intake",
    ]
    for col in numeric_cols:
        if col in out.columns:
            out[f"{col}_missing"] = out[col].isna().astype("int8")
            out[col] = pd.to_numeric(out[col], errors="coerce")

    eps = 1e-6
    if {"step_count", "exercise_duration"}.issubset(out.columns):
        out["steps_per_exercise_minute"] = out["step_count"] / (out["exercise_duration"] + eps)
    if {"calorie_expenditure", "exercise_duration"}.issubset(out.columns):
        out["calories_per_exercise_minute"] = out["calorie_expenditure"] / (out["exercise_duration"] + eps)
    if {"sleep_duration", "sleep_quality"}.issubset(out.columns):
        sleep_quality_score = out["sleep_quality"].map({"poor": 0.0, "average": 1.0, "good": 2.0})
        out["sleep_duration_quality"] = out["sleep_duration"] * sleep_quality_score
    if {"bmi", "heart_rate"}.issubset(out.columns):
        out["bmi_heart_rate"] = out["bmi"] * out["heart_rate"]
    return out
```
